acceptanceCriteria.criterion: A streamed agent message cannot create one permanent AuditEvent per token or text delta
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: High-frequency delta methods are aggregated before durable recording.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: One long turn produces bounded telemetry events
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: Recovered telemetry is compacted and live telemetry is emitted in bounded aggregate windows.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Final agent messages and all command/tool lifecycle events remain reconstructible
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: Only delta telemetry is aggregated; lifecycle, final, error and command events retain durable event contracts.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Restart flushes aggregates without duplication or loss
acceptanceCriteria.evidence: Recovered pending telemetry compacts and rebases onto the confirmed remote chain; live restart drained durable events with receipts.
Compacted and rebased events are rehashed through CanonicalJson and accepted by game-api.
Process-wide circuit is shared by tracker polling, heartbeat, lifecycle and outbox calls; Retry-After/reset is honored with bounded jitter.
make all passed on exact publishable branch head with 278 tests, 0 failures, 2 skipped, 100% coverage, Credo clean and Dialyzer 0 errors.

checks.command: cd elixir && mise exec -- make all
checks.summary: 278 tests, 0 failures, 2 skipped; 100% coverage; format/spec/build passed; Credo clean; Dialyzer 0 errors.
After restart, run_91_1784604071684 resumed at the same branch and commit; pending events fell from 213 to 5 with 33 confirmed receipts before new live events arrived.
Vercel production logs showed zero 429 responses during the post-fix recovery window; normal 200 responses resumed and optimistic 409 audit conflicts reconciled.

residualRisk.summary: The circuit is process-local by design because the current pilot has one X1 runner; a future multi-runner deployment will require a distributed coordination policy.

summary: Bounded audit telemetry, paced durable recovery, and a shared rate-limit circuit are verified on the publishable Symphony branch and in the live X1 runtime.
